text.title: active-listening Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for active-listening as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Generating empathic reflection statements without knowing the interviewee's emotional context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Statements like "It sounds like you're frustrated..." land as condescending or inaccurate when the person was calm. Reflection statements must respond to observed signals, not be pre-generated.

failure-mode.description: Using "What" or "How" openers for questions that still embed assumptions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "What problems did you have with X?" assumes X caused problems. Check every open question for embedded assumptions before the session.

failure-mode.description: Replacing empathic presence with agent-generated scripts in high-stakes conflict conversations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Using an agent-generated script in a sensitive interpersonal situation risks mechanical delivery that backfires. Active listening is fundamentally reactive; agents cannot adapt to real-time emotional signals.
